text: In order to grow and develop, arthropods need to molt periodically (Heming 2018). Since molting is a determining point in arthropod development, the disruption of molting leads to increased mortality (Arakawa et al. 2008; Merzendorfer et al. 2012; Song et al. 2017a; Song et al. 2017b). During ecdysis, arthropods pause food intake and respiration (Camp et al. 2014; Song et al. 2017a). Therefore, if the molt cannot be completed, the organism may die of starvation or suffocation. Additionally, if the cuticle is immature, it may not withstand the stresses associated with ecdysis (Clarke 1957; Lee 1961; Dall et al. 1978; deFur et al. 1985), and the organism may die of desiccation or increased susceptibility to pathogens. Given the well understood biological processes, the biological plausibility of this KER was rated as high.
